revision.comment: move the default to the second line
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined.

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23168229401|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

* mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Citation_Style_documentation%2Fdisplay here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: * mode: Sets element separator, default terminal punctuation, and certain capitalization according to the value provided. For {{para|mode|cs1}}, element separator and terminal punctuation is a period (.); where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are capitalized ('Retrieved...'). For {{para|mode|cs2}}, element separator is a comma (,); terminal punctuation is omitted; where appropriate, initial letters of certain words are not capitalized ('retrieved...'). These styles correspond to Citation Style 1 and Citation Style 2 respectively. To override default terminal punctuation use '''postscript'''. * author-mask: * contributor-mask: * editor-mask: * interviewer-mask: * subject-mask: * translator-mask: *: Replaces the name of the (first) author with [[dash#Em dash|em dashes]] or text. Set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a numeric value ''n'' to set the dash ''n'' em spaces wide; set '''<{{var|name}}>-mask''' to a text value to display the text without a trailing author separator; for example, "with". The numeric value 0 is a special case to be used in conjunction with '''<{{var|name}}>-link'''—in this case, the value of '''<{{var|name}}>-link''' will be used as (linked) text. In either case, you must still include the values for all names for metadata purposes. Primarily intended for use with bibliographies or bibliography styles where multiple works by a single author are listed sequentially such as {{sfnote|lc=y}}. Do not use in a list generated by {{tlx|reflist}}, {{tag|references|s}} or similar as there is no control of the order in which references are displayed. Mask parameters can take an enumerator in the name of the parameter (e.g. {{para|author{{var|n}}-mask}}) to apply the mask to a specific {{var|name}}. * display-authors: * display-contributors: * display-editors: * display-interviewers: * display-subjects: * display-translators: *: Controls the number of author (or other kind of contributor) names that are displayed. By default, all authors are displayed. To change the displayed number of names, set the parameter to the desired number. For example, {{para|display-authors|2}} will display only the first two authors in a citation (and not affect the display of the other kinds of contributors). {{para|display-authors|0}} is a special case suppressing the display of all authors including the et al. {{para|display-authors|etal}} displays all authors in the list followed by et al. Aliases: none. * postscript: Controls the closing punctuation for a citation; defaults to {{#if: {{{cs2|}}}|none|a period (.); for no terminating punctuation, specify {{para|postscript|none}} – leaving {{para|postscript}} empty is the same as omitting it, but is ambiguous}}. Additional text, or templates that render more than a single terminating punctuation character, will generate a maintenance message. {{para|postscript}} is ignored if '''quote''' is defined. [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
